contentBlocks.original:
श्री श्रीनिवासा गोविन्दा श्री वेङ्कटेशा गोविन्दा
भक्तवत्सला गोविन्दा भागवतप्रिय गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī śrīnivāsā gōvindā śrī vēṅkaṭēśā gōvindā
bhaktavatsalā gōvindā bhāgavatapriya gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who dwells in Śrī (Śrīnivāsa), O Govinda who is Lord of Vēṅkaṭa hill! O Govinda who cherishes his devotees, O Govinda beloved of the Bhāgavatas! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā, Vēṅkaṭaramaṇa Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-śrīnivāsa = one in whom Śrī (Lakṣmī) abides; vēṅkaṭēśa = lord of Vēṅkaṭa hill; bhaktavatsala = tender toward devotees (vatsala = affectionate like a cow to its calf); bhāgavatapriya = dear to the Bhāgavatas (devotees of Viṣṇu).

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यनिर्मला गोविन्दा नीलमेघश्याम गोविन्दा
पुराणपुरुषा गोविन्दा पुण्डरीकाक्ष गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityanirmalā gōvindā nīlamēghaśyāma gōvindā
purāṇapuruṣā gōvindā puṇḍarīkākṣa gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda eternally pure, O Govinda dark as the blue monsoon cloud! O Govinda the Primeval Being of the Purāṇas, O Govinda of lotus-eyes! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: nityanirmala = eternally stainless/pure; nīlameghaśyāma = dark (śyāma) as the blue (nīla) rain-cloud (megha); purāṇapuruṣa = the Primeval Person of the Purāṇas; puṇḍarīkākṣa = lotus-eyed (puṇḍarīka = white lotus; akṣa = eye).

contentBlocks.original:
नन्दनन्दना गोविन्दा नवनीतचोरा गोविन्दा
पशुपालक श्री गोविन्दा पापविमोचन गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nandanandanā gōvindā navanītachōrā gōvindā
paśupālaka śrī gōvindā pāpavimōchana gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the son of Nanda, O Govinda the stealer of fresh butter! O auspicious Govinda the herdsman of cows, O Govinda the liberator from sin! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: nandanandana = son (nandana) of Nanda; navanītacora = stealer (cora) of fresh butter (navanīta); paśupālaka = protector/herdsman (pālaka) of cattle (paśu); pāpavimocana = liberator (vimocana) from sins (pāpa).

contentBlocks.original:
दुष्टसंहार गोविन्दा दुरितनिवारण गोविन्दा
शिष्टपरिपालक गोविन्दा कष्टनिवारण गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
duṣṭasaṃhāra gōvindā duritanivāraṇa gōvindā
śiṣṭaparipālaka gōvindā kaṣṭanivāraṇa gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the destroyer of the wicked, O Govinda the remover of sins! O Govinda the protector of the righteous, O Govinda the dispeller of hardships! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: duṣṭasaṃhāra = destroyer (saṃhāra) of the wicked (duṣṭa); duritanivāraṇa = remover (nivāraṇa) of sins/evil (durita); śiṣṭaparipālaka = complete protector (paripālaka) of the good/virtuous (śiṣṭa); kaṣṭanivāraṇa = dispeller (nivāraṇa) of hardships/sufferings (kaṣṭa).

contentBlocks.original:
वज्रमकुटधर गोविन्दा वराहमूर्तिवि गोविन्दा
गोपीजनप्रिय गोविन्दा गोवर्धनोद्धार गोविन्दा [गोपीजनलोल]
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vajramakuṭadhara gōvindā varāhamūrtivi gōvindā
gōpījanapriya gōvindā gōvardhanōddhāra gōvindā [gōpījanalōla]
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who wears the diamond crown, O Govinda who took the form of the divine Boar! O Govinda beloved of the gopīs, O Govinda who lifted Mount Govardhana! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: vajramakuṭadhara = one who wears (dhara) a crown (makuṭa) of diamond (vajra); varāhamūrti = one whose form (mūrti) is the Boar (Varāha); gopījanapriya = beloved (priya) of the gopī women; govardhanoddāra = lifter (uddāra) of Mount Govardhana.

contentBlocks.original:
दशरथनन्दन गोविन्दा दशमुखमर्दन गोविन्दा
पक्षिवाहना गोविन्दा पाण्डवप्रिय गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśarathanandana gōvindā daśamukhamardana gōvindā
pakṣivāhanā gōvindā pāṇḍavapriya gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the son of Daśaratha, O Govinda the slayer of ten-headed Rāvaṇa! O Govinda who rides the king of birds, O Govinda beloved of the Pāṇḍavas! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: daśarathanandana = son (nandana) of King Daśaratha (i.e., Rāma); daśamukhamardana = crusher (mardana) of the ten-faced (daśamukha) Rāvaṇa; pakṣivāhana = one whose vehicle (vāhana) is a bird (pakṣī), i.e., Garuḍa; pāṇḍavapriya = beloved (priya) of the Pāṇḍava brothers.

contentBlocks.original:
मत्स्यकूर्म गोविन्दा मधुसूधन हरि गोविन्दा
वराह नरसिंह गोविन्दा वामन भृगुराम गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
matsyakūrma gōvindā madhusūdhana hari gōvindā
varāha narasiṃha gōvindā vāmana bhṛgurāma gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who appeared as Fish and Tortoise, O Govinda who is Hari the slayer of Madhu! O Govinda who appeared as Boar and Man-Lion, O Govinda who appeared as Vāmana and Paraśurāma! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: matsyakūrma = Fish (matsya) and Tortoise (kūrma) avatāras; madhusūdhana = slayer (sūdhana) of the demon Madhu; varāha narasiṃha = Boar and Man-Lion avatāras; vāmana bhṛgurāma = the Dwarf (Vāmana) and Paraśurāma (of Bhṛgu's lineage).

contentBlocks.original:
बलरामानुज गोविन्दा बौद्ध कल्किधर गोविन्दा
वेणुगानप्रिय गोविन्दा वेङ्कटरमणा गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
balarāmānuja gōvindā bauddha kalkidhara gōvindā
vēṇugānapriya gōvindā vēṅkaṭaramaṇā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the younger brother of Balarāma, O Govinda who appeared as Buddha and will come as Kalki! O Govinda who loves the music of the flute, O Govinda who is the delight of Vēṅkaṭa! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: balarāmānuja = younger brother (anuja) of Balarāma; bauddha kalkidhara = one who held the forms of Buddha and will hold Kalki; veṇugānapriya = lover (priya) of flute-music (veṇugāna); vēṅkaṭaramaṇa = delight/joy (ramaṇa) of Vēṅkaṭa hill.

contentBlocks.original:
सीतानायक गोविन्दा श्रितपरिपालक गोविन्दा
श्रितजनपोषक गोविन्दा धर्मसंस्थापक गोविन्दा [दरिद्रजन पोषक]
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sītānāyaka gōvindā śritaparipālaka gōvindā
śritajanapōṣaka gōvindā dharmasaṃsthāpaka gōvindā [daridrajana pōṣaka]
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the lord of Sītā, O Govinda the protector of those who take refuge! O Govinda the nourisher of those who seek shelter, O Govinda the establisher of dharma! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: sītānāyaka = lord/husband (nāyaka) of Sītā; śritaparipālaka = complete protector (paripālaka) of those who take refuge (śrita); śritajanopōṣaka = nourisher (pōṣaka) of people (jana) who seek shelter (śrita); dharmasaṃsthāpaka = establisher (saṃsthāpaka) of dharma.

contentBlocks.original:
अनाथरक्षक गोविन्दा आपद्भान्दव गोविन्दा
भक्तवत्सला गोविन्दा करुणासागर गोविन्दा [शरणागतवत्सल]
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anātharakṣaka gōvindā āpadbhāndava gōvindā
bhaktavatsalā gōvindā karuṇāsāgara gōvindā [śaraṇāgatavatsala]
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the protector of the helpless, O Govinda the kinsman in times of calamity! O Govinda who is tender toward devotees, O Govinda the ocean of compassion! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: anātharakṣaka = protector (rakṣaka) of the forlorn/helpless (anātha); āpadbhāndava = kinsman/friend (bāndava) in calamity (āpad); bhaktavatsala = affectionate toward devotees (vatsala = tender as a cow to its calf); karuṇāsāgara = ocean (sāgara) of compassion (karuṇā).

contentBlocks.original:
कमलदलाक्ष गोविन्दा कामितफलदात गोविन्दा
पापविनाशक गोविन्दा पाहि मुरारे गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kamaladaḻākṣa gōvindā kāmitaphaladāta gōvindā
pāpavināśaka gōvindā pāhi murārē gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda with lotus-petal eyes, O Govinda who grants all desired fruits! O Govinda who destroys sins, protect us, O Govinda the slayer of Mura! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: kamaladaḻākṣa = one whose eyes (akṣa) are lotus-petals (kamala-daḻa); kāmitaphaladāta = giver (dāta) of desired (kāmita) fruits (phala); pāpavināśaka = destroyer (vināśaka) of sins (pāpa); pāhi murāre = protect (pāhi) us, O slayer of Mura (Murāri = Kṛṣṇa who killed demon Mura).

contentBlocks.original:
श्री मुद्राङ्कित गोविन्दा श्री वत्साङ्कित गोविन्दा
धरणीनायक गोविन्दा दिनकरतेजा गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī mudrāṅkita gōvindā śrī vatsāṅkita gōvindā
dharaṇīnāyaka gōvindā dinakaratējā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda marked with the sign of Śrī (Lakṣmī's imprint), O Govinda who bears the Śrīvatsa curl! O Govinda the lord of the earth, O Govinda brilliant as the sun! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-mudrāṅkita = one marked (aṅkita) with Śrī's seal/imprint (mudrā); śrī-vatsāṅkita = one bearing (aṅkita) the Śrīvatsa mark (an auspicious whorl of hair on Viṣṇu's chest, said to be the eternal dwelling of Śrī); dharaṇīnāyaka = lord (nāyaka) of the earth (dharaṇī); dinakaratejas = possessing the brilliance (tejas) of the sun (dinakara).

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मावतीप्रिय गोविन्दा प्रसन्नमूर्ती गोविन्दा
अभयहस्त गोविन्दा मत्स्यावतार गोविन्दा [प्रदर्शक]
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmāvatīpriya gōvindā prasannamūrtī gōvindā
abhayahasta gōvindā matsyāvatāra gōvindā [pradarśaka]
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda beloved of Padmāvatī, O Govinda of gracious and serene form! O Govinda who extends the gesture of fearlessness, O Govinda who descended as the Fish! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: padmāvatīpriya = beloved (priya) of Padmāvatī (the goddess of Tirumala, Viṣṇu's consort in his Vēṅkaṭēśvara form); prasannamūrti = one of gracious, pleased form (prasanna = serene, kindly); abhayahasta = one whose hand (hasta) grants freedom from fear (abhaya); matsyāvatāra = the Fish descent (avatāra) of Viṣṇu.

contentBlocks.original:
शङ्खचक्रधर गोविन्दा शार्​ङ्गगदाधर गोविन्दा
विराजातीर्धस्थ गोविन्दा विरोधिमर्धन गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaṅkhachakradhara gōvindā śār​ṅgagadādhara gōvindā
virājātīrdhastha gōvindā virōdhimardhana gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who bears conch and discus, O Govinda who wields the bow Śārṅga and the mace! O Govinda who abides at the Virājā-tīrtha, O Govinda who crushes adversaries! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: śaṅkhachakradhara = bearer (dhara) of the conch Pāñcajanya (śaṅkha) and discus Sudarśana (cakra); śārṅgagadādhara = bearer of the bow Śārṅga and the mace Kaumodakī (gadā); virājātīrthastha = one who stands (stha) at the Virājā-tīrtha (the sacred river at the boundary of Vaikuṇṭha, also a tīrtha near Puri); virodhimardana = crusher (mardana) of enemies/adversaries (virodhin).

contentBlocks.original:
सालग्राम[धर] गोविन्दा सहस्रनामा गोविन्दा
लक्ष्मीवल्लभ गोविन्दा लक्ष्मणाग्रज गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sālagrāma[dhara] gōvindā sahasranāmā gōvindā
lakṣmīvallabha gōvindā lakṣmaṇāgraja gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda enshrined in the Śālagrāma stone, O Govinda of a thousand names! O Govinda the beloved consort of Lakṣmī, O Govinda the elder brother of Lakṣmaṇa! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: sālagrāmadhara = one enshrined/bearing (dhara) the Śālagrāma (an ammonite fossil sacred to Viṣṇu, worshipped as his aniconic form); sahasranāma = one of a thousand (sahasra) names (nāma); lakṣmīvallabha = beloved husband (vallabha) of Lakṣmī; lakṣmaṇāgraja = elder brother (agraja) of Lakṣmaṇa (as Rāma).

contentBlocks.original:
कस्तूरितिलक गोविन्दा कनकाम्बरधर गोविन्दा [काञ्चनाम्बरधर]
गरुडवाहना गोविन्दा गजराज रक्षक गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kastūritilaka gōvindā kanakāmbaradhara gōvindā [kāñchanāmbaradhara]
garuḍavāhanā gōvindā gajarāja rakṣaka gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda wearing the musk-dot on his forehead, O Govinda clad in golden robes! O Govinda who rides Garuḍa the eagle-king, O Govinda the guardian of Gokula! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: kastūritilaka = one adorned with a tilaka (forehead mark) of musk (kastūrī); kanakāmbaradhara = one who wears (dhara) golden (kanaka) garments (ambara); garuḍavāhana = one whose mount (vāhana) is Garuḍa the eagle-king; gokulpālaka = guardian/protector (pālaka) of Gokula.

contentBlocks.original:
वानरसेवित गोविन्दा वारधिबन्धन गोविन्दा
एकस्वरूप गोविन्दा रामकृष्णा गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vānarasēvita gōvindā vāradhibandhana gōvindā
ēkasvarūpa gōvindā rāmakṛṣṇā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda served by the monkey army, O Govinda who bridged the ocean! O Govinda who is one undivided Reality, O Govinda who is both Rāma and Kṛṣṇa! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: vānarasevita = served (sevita) by the vānara (monkey) army; vāradhibandhana = one who built the bridge (bandhana = binding/bridge) across the ocean (vāridhi/vāradhi); ekasvarūpa = one of single (eka) form/nature (svarūpa), i.e., non-dual Reality; rāmakṛṣṇa = both Rāma and Kṛṣṇa (two avatāras who are one).

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तनन्दन गोविन्दा प्रत्यक्षदेवा गोविन्दा
परमदयाकर गोविन्दा वज्रकवचधर गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktanandana gōvindā pratyakṣadēvā gōvindā
paramadayākara gōvindā vajrakavachadhara gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who delights his devotees, O Govinda who is the directly manifest deity! O Govinda of supreme compassion, O Govinda who wears the diamond armor! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhaktanandana = one who brings joy (nandana) to devotees (bhakta); pratyakṣadeva = the directly (pratyakṣa = perceptible, evident) manifest God (deva), a title prized at Tirumala where Vēṅkaṭēśvara is considered svayam-vyakta (self-manifested, not installed by human hands); paramadayākara = one who bestows (kara) supreme (parama) compassion (dayā); vajrakavachadhara = one who wears (dhara) a diamond (vajra) armor (kavaca).

contentBlocks.original:
वैजयन्तिमाल गोविन्दा वड्डिकासुल गोविन्दा
वसुदेवसुत गोविन्दा श्रीवासुदेव गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaijayantimāla gōvindā vaḍḍikāsula gōvindā
vasudēvasuta gōvindā śrīvāsudēva gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda wearing the Vaijayantī garland, O Govinda lord of the devoted throngs! O Govinda the son of Vasudeva, O Govinda the blessed Vāsudeva! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: vaijayantīmāla = one wearing the Vaijayantī garland (made of five types of gems or flowers, a mark of Viṣṇu); vaḍḍikāsula = in Telugu, those deeply indebted/bonded (vaḍḍi = interest; the compound suggests devoted suppliants who owe everything to the Lord); vasudēvasuta = son (suta) of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa's biological father); śrī-vāsudeva = the blessed (śrī) Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa as son of Vasudeva, or the cosmic Vāsudeva of the Pāñcarātra tradition).

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यकल्याण गोविन्दा नीरजनाभ गोविन्दा
नीलाद्रिवास गोविन्दा नीलमेघश्याम गोविन्दा [क्षीराब्ढिवास]
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityakaḻyāṇa gōvindā nīrajanābha gōvindā
nīlādrivāsa gōvindā nīlamēghaśyāma gōvindā [kṣīrābḍhivāsa]
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda of eternal auspiciousness, O Govinda from whose navel blooms the lotus! O Govinda who dwells on the blue mountain, O Govinda dark as the blue monsoon cloud! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: nityakalyāṇa = one of eternal (nitya) auspiciousness (kalyāṇa); nīrajanābha = one whose navel (nābha) is a water-lily/lotus (nīraja = born of water); nīlādrivāsa = one who dwells (vāsa) on the blue mountain (nīlādri), another name for Tirumala (the 'blue hill'); nīlameghaśyāma = dark (śyāma) as the blue (nīla) rain-cloud (megha).

contentBlocks.original:
स्वयं प्ऱकश गोविन्दा आनम्दनिलय गोविन्दा
स्ऱीदेविनाठ गोविन्दा देवकि नन्दन गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svayaṃ pRakaśa gōvindā ānamdanilaya gōvindā
sRīdēvināṭha gōvindā dēvaki nandana gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who is self-luminous, O Govinda who is the abode of bliss! O Govinda the lord of Śrīdevī, O Govinda the joy of mother Devakī! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: svayaṃprakāśa = self-luminous (svayam = self; prakāśa = light/radiance), one who illumines by his own nature without being illumined by another; ānandalaya = abode/refuge (laya = resting place) of bliss (ānanda); śrīdevīnātha = lord (nātha) of Śrīdevī (Lakṣmī); devakīnandana = one who brought joy (nandana) to Devakī (Kṛṣṇa's birth-mother).

contentBlocks.original:
तिरुमलवास गोविन्दा रत्नकिरीट गोविन्दा
आश्रितपक्ष गोविन्दा नित्यशुभप्रद गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tirumalavāsa gōvindā ratnakirīṭa gōvindā
āśritapakṣa gōvindā nityaśubhaprada gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who dwells at Tirumala, O Govinda wearing the jeweled crown! O Govinda who takes the side of those who seek refuge, O Govinda who grants eternal auspiciousness! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: tirumalavāsa = one who dwells (vāsa) at Tirumala (the sacred seven hills of Vēṅkaṭēśvara); ratnakirīṭa = one wearing a crown (kirīṭa) of gems/jewels (ratna); āśritapakṣa = one who sides (pakṣa = side, wing) with those who take refuge (āśrita); nityaśubhaprada = one who bestows (prada) eternal (nitya) auspiciousness/welfare (śubha).

contentBlocks.original:
आनन्दरूप गोविन्दा आद्यन्तरहित गोविन्दा
इहपर दायक गोविन्दा इभराज रक्षक गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānandarūpa gōvindā ādyantarahita gōvindā
ihapara dāyaka gōvindā ibharāja rakṣaka gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda of the form of bliss, O Govinda who is without beginning or end! O Govinda who grants blessings here and hereafter, O Govinda who saved the elephant king! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: ānandarūpa = one of the form (rūpa) of bliss (ānanda); ādyantarahita = one devoid of (rahita) beginning (ādi) and end (anta), i.e., eternal; ihapara-dāyaka = one who bestows (dāyaka) benefits both here (iha) and hereafter (para); ibharāja-rakṣaka = protector (rakṣaka) of the elephant king (ibharāja = ibha + rāja) — referring to Gajendra, the elephant who was rescued from a crocodile.

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मदलाक्ष गोविन्दा तिरुमलनिल्य गोविन्दा
शेषशायिनी गोविन्दा शेषाद्रिनिलय गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmadalākṣa gōvindā tirumalanilya gōvindā
śēṣaśāyinī gōvindā śēṣādrinilaya gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda with lotus-petal eyes, O Govinda who makes his home at Tirumala! O Govinda who reclines on the cosmic Śeṣa serpent, O Govinda who dwells on Śeṣādri hill! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: padmadalākṣa = one whose eyes (akṣa) are like lotus-petals (padma-daḻa); tirumalanilaya = one whose abode (nilaya) is Tirumala; śeṣaśāyinī = one who lies (śāyin) on Śeṣa (the thousand-headed cosmic serpent, also called Ananta — the Endless); śeṣādrinilaya = one who dwells on Śeṣādri (the 'Śeṣa mountain,' another name for Tirumala, said to be the coiled body of the serpent Ādiśeṣa).

contentBlocks.original:
वराह ॠप गोविन्दा श्री खूर्मरूप गोविन्दा
वामनॠप गोविन्दा नरहरिॠप गोविन्दा [हरिहरॠप]
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
varāha ṝpa gōvindā śrī khūrmarūpa gōvindā
vāmanaṝpa gōvindā narahariṝpa gōvindā [hariharaṝpa]
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda in the form of the divine Boar, O Govinda in the auspicious form of the Tortoise! O Govinda in the form of the Dwarf, O Govinda in the form of the Man-Lion! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: varāharūpa = one of the form (rūpa) of Varāha (the Boar who rescued the Earth); śrī-kūrmarūpa = one of the auspicious (śrī) form of Kūrma (the Tortoise who bore Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean); vāmanarūpa = one of the form of Vāmana (the Dwarf who reclaimed the three worlds from the demon king Bali in three strides); naharirūpa = one of the form of Narahari (Man-Lion, who tore apart Hiraṇyakaśipu).

contentBlocks.original:
श्री परशुराम गोविन्दा श्री बलराम गोविन्दा
रघुकुल राम गोविन्दा श्री रामकृष्ण गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī paraśurāma gōvindā śrī balarāma gōvindā
raghukula rāma gōvindā śrī rāmakṛṣṇa gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who appeared as the blessed Paraśurāma, O Govinda who appeared as the blessed Balarāma! O Govinda who is Rāma of the Raghu dynasty, O Govinda who is the blessed Rāma and Kṛṣṇa! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-paraśurāma = the blessed warrior-sage Paraśurāma (Viṣṇu's sixth avatāra, wielder of the axe paraśu, who cleared the earth of corrupt Kṣatriyas 21 times); śrī-balarāma = the blessed Balarāma (Kṛṣṇa's elder brother, the seventh avatāra, wielder of the plough and mace); raghukula-rāma = Rāma of the Raghu lineage (the Solar dynasty); śrī-rāmakṛṣṇa = the blessed Rāma-and-Kṛṣṇa (the eighth and ninth avatāras united).

contentBlocks.original:
तिरुमलनायक गोविन्दा श्रितजनपोषक गोविन्दा
श्रीदेविनाठ गोविन्दा श्रीवत्साङ्कित गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tirumalanāyaka gōvindā śritajanapōṣaka gōvindā
śrīdēvināṭha gōvindā śrīvatsāṅkita gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda the lord of Tirumala, O Govinda who nourishes those who seek shelter! O Govinda the master of Śrīdevī, O Govinda who bears the Śrīvatsa mark! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: tirumalanāyaka = ruler/lord (nāyaka) of Tirumala; śritajana-pōṣaka = nourisher (pōṣaka) of people (jana) who take shelter (śrita); śrīdevīnātha = lord (nātha) of Śrīdevī (Mahālakṣmī); śrīvatsāṅkita = one bearing (aṅkita) the Śrīvatsa (the eternal mark on Viṣṇu's chest that serves as Lakṣmī's perpetual abode).

contentBlocks.original:
गोविन्दानाम गोविन्दा वेङ्कटरमणा गोविन्दा
क्षॆत्रपालक गोविन्दा तिरुमलनथ गोविन्दा ।
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gōvindānāma gōvindā vēṅkaṭaramaṇā gōvindā
kṣetrapālaka gōvindā tirumalanatha gōvindā ।
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda whose very name is Govinda, O Govinda the delight of Vēṅkaṭa! O Govinda the guardian of the sacred kṣetra, O Govinda the lord of Tirumala! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: govindānāma = one whose name (nāma) is Govinda itself; vēṅkaṭaramaṇa = the delight (ramaṇa = one who revels, lover) of Vēṅkaṭa; kṣetrapālaka = guardian/protector (pālaka) of the sacred kṣetra (pilgrimage site); tirumalanātha = lord (nātha) of Tirumala.

contentBlocks.original:
वानरसेवित गोविन्दा वारधिबन्धन गोविन्दा
एडुकॊण्डलवाड गोविन्दा एकत्वरूपा गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vānarasēvita gōvindā vāradhibandhana gōvindā
ēḍukoṇḍalavāḍa gōvindā ēkatvarūpā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda served by the monkey hosts, O Govinda who built the bridge across the ocean! O Govinda who dwells among the seven hills, O Govinda who is the form of Oneness! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: vānarasevita = attended/served (sevita) by the vānara (monkey) hosts; vāradhibandhana = one who bound/bridged (bandhana) the ocean (vāradhi); ēḍukoṇḍalavāḍa = one who dwells (vāḍa) among the seven hills (ēḍu-koṇḍalu — the seven peaks of Tirumala in Telugu); ekatvārūpa = one of the form (rūpa) of Oneness (ekatva).

contentBlocks.original:
श्री रामकृष्णा गोविन्दा रघुकुल नन्दन गोविन्दा
प्रत्यक्षदेवा गोविन्दा परमदयाकर गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī rāmakṛṣṇā gōvindā raghukula nandana gōvindā
pratyakṣadēvā gōvindā paramadayākara gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who is the blessed Rāma and Kṛṣṇa, O Govinda the pride of the Raghu lineage! O Govinda the directly manifest deity, O Govinda of supreme compassion! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-rāmakṛṣṇa = the blessed Rāma and Kṛṣṇa (recurring pairing affirming avatāra unity); raghukula-nandana = delight (nandana) of the Raghu dynasty; pratyakṣadeva = directly manifest (pratyakṣa) God (deva) — self-revealed at Tirumala; paramadayākara = bestower (kara) of supreme (parama) compassion (dayā).

contentBlocks.original:
वज्रकवचधर गोविन्दा वैजयन्तिमाल गोविन्दा
वड्डिकासुलवाड गोविन्दा वसुदेवतनया गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vajrakavachadhara gōvindā vaijayantimāla gōvindā
vaḍḍikāsulavāḍa gōvindā vasudēvatanayā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who wears the diamond armor, O Govinda wearing the Vaijayantī garland! O Govinda lord of the devoted throngs of Vaḍḍikāsulu, O Govinda the son of Vasudeva! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: vajrakavachadhara = wearer (dhara) of the diamond (vajra) armor (kavaca); vaijayantīmāla = one wearing the Vaijayantī garland (five-jewel or five-flower garland symbolic of the five elements, a signature ornament of Viṣṇu); vaḍḍikāsulavāḍa = one who dwells among (vāḍa) the Vaḍḍikāsulu (devoted suppliants of Tirumala, Telugu word for those bound to the Lord); vasudeva-tanaya = son (tanaya) of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa's father).

contentBlocks.original:
बिल्वपत्रार्चित गोविन्दा भिक्षुक संस्तुत गोविन्दा
स्त्रीपुंसरूपा गोविन्दा शिवकेशवमूर्ति गोविन्दा
ब्रह्माण्डरूपा गोविन्दा भक्तरक्षक गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bilvapatrārchita gōvindā bhikṣuka saṃstuta gōvindā
strīpuṃsarūpā gōvindā śivakēśavamūrti gōvindā
brahmāṇḍarūpā gōvindā bhaktarakṣaka gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda worshipped with bilva leaves, O Govinda praised by wandering ascetics! O Govinda who embodies both female and male, O Govinda the friend of Śiva! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: bilvapatrārcita = one worshipped (arcita) with bilva leaves (bilvapatras, sacred to both Śiva and Viṣṇu); bhikṣuka-saṃstuta = praised (saṃstuta) by bhikṣukas (wandering mendicants/ascetics); strīpuṃsarūpa = one of the form (rūpa) that encompasses both female (strī) and male (puṃs), i.e., the androgynous completeness of the divine; śivasakha = friend (sakha) of Śiva.

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यकल्याण गोविन्दा नीरजनाभ गोविन्दा
हातीरामप्रिय गोविन्दा हरि सर्वोत्तम गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityakaḻyāṇa gōvindā nīrajanābha gōvindā
hātīrāmapriya gōvindā hari sarvōttama gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda of eternal auspiciousness, O Govinda from whose navel the lotus blooms! O Govinda beloved of saint Hātīrāma, O Govinda Hari who is supreme above all! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: nityakalyāṇa = of eternal (nitya) auspiciousness (kalyāṇa); nīrajanābha = lotus (nīraja = water-born) at the navel (nābha), from which Brahmā emerged; hātīrāmapriya = beloved (priya) of Hātīrāma Bhaṭṭu (the Telangana saint-devotee who lived at Tirumala in the 17th century and is said to have received the Lord's direct darśana through his devoted service); hari-sarvottama = Hari who is supreme (sarvottama = highest of all).

contentBlocks.original:
जनार्धनमूर्ति गोविन्दा जगत्साक्षिरूपा गोविन्दा
अभिषेकप्रिय गोविन्दा आपन्निवारण गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
janārdhanamūrti gōvindā jagatsākṣirūpā gōvindā
abhiṣēkapriya gōvindā āpannivāraṇa gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda of the form of Janārdana, O Govinda who is the witness of all worlds! O Govinda who delights in sacred abhiṣeka, O Govinda who wards off calamities! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: janārdhanamūrti = one of the form (mūrti) of Janārdana (he who sends people to liberation — one who harrows/compels; or, one who is prayed to by people); jagatsākṣirūpa = one whose form (rūpa) is the witness (sākṣin) of all the worlds (jagat); abhiṣekapriya = one who delights (priya) in the abhiṣeka ceremony (ritual anointing with sacred waters, milk, honey, etc.); āpannivāra = one who wards off (nivāra) calamities/afflictions (āpad).

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नकिरीटा गोविन्दा रामानुजनुत गोविन्दा
स्वयम्प्रकाशा गोविन्दा आश्रितपक्ष गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnakirīṭā gōvindā rāmānujanuta gōvindā
svayamprakāśā gōvindā āśritapakṣa gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda wearing the jeweled crown, O Govinda praised by the saint Rāmānuja! O Govinda who is self-luminous, O Govinda who takes the side of those who seek refuge! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: ratnakirīṭa = one wearing a crown (kirīṭa) of gems (ratna); rāmānujanuta = one praised (nuta = saluted, extolled) by Rāmānuja (the 11th-12th century Vaiṣṇava philosopher-saint and founder of Viśiṣṭādvaita Vedānta, who visited Tirumala); svayamprakāśa = self-luminous (svayam = by itself; prakāśa = light); āśritapakṣa = one who sides (pakṣa = party, wing) with those who take refuge (āśrita).

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यशुभप्रद गोविन्दा निखिललोकेशा गोविन्दा
आनन्दरूपा गोविन्दा आद्यन्तरहिता गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityaśubhaprada gōvindā nikhilalōkēśā gōvindā
ānandarūpā gōvindā ādyantarahitā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who bestows perpetual auspiciousness, O Govinda the lord of all worlds! O Govinda of the form of bliss, O Govinda who transcends beginning and end! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: nityaśubhaprada = one who bestows (prada) perpetual (nitya) auspiciousness/welfare (śubha); nikhilalōkēśa = lord (īśa) of all (nikhila) worlds (loka); ānandarūpa = of the form (rūpa) of bliss (ānanda); ādyantarahita = devoid of (rahita) beginning (ādi) and end (anta), i.e., eternal.

contentBlocks.original:
इहपर दायक गोविन्दा इभराज रक्षक गोविन्दा
परमदयालो गोविन्दा पद्मनाभहरि गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ihapara dāyaka gōvindā ibharāja rakṣaka gōvindā
paramadayāḻō gōvindā padmanābhahari gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who grants blessings here and hereafter, O Govinda who rescued the elephant king! O Govinda of supreme compassion, O Govinda the lotus-naveled Hari! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: ihapara-dāyaka = bestower (dāyaka) of blessings both here (iha = in this world) and hereafter (para = in the next); ibharāja-rakṣaka = protector (rakṣaka) of Ibharāja — the elephant king (ibha = elephant; rāja = king), referring to Gajendra; paramadayāla = supremely compassionate (paramadayālu — Telugu form of paramadayālu); padmanābha-hari = the lotus-naveled (padmanābha) Hari (Viṣṇu), from whose navel the lotus bearing Brahmā emerged at creation.

contentBlocks.original:
तिरुमलवासा गोविन्दा तुलसीवनमाल गोविन्दा
शेषाद्रिनिलया गोविन्दा शेषसायिनी गोविन्दा
श्री श्रीनिवासा गोविन्दा श्री वेङ्कटेशा गोविन्दा
गोविन्दा हरि गोविन्दा गोकुलनन्दन गोविन्दा [वेङ्कटरमण] ॥ 38 ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tirumalavāsā gōvindā tulasīvanamāla gōvindā
śēṣādrinilayā gōvindā śēṣasāyinī gōvindā
śrī śrīnivāsā gōvindā śrī vēṅkaṭēśā gōvindā
gōvindā hari gōvindā gōkulanandana gōvindā [vēṅkaṭaramaṇa] ॥ 38 ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Govinda who dwells at Tirumala, O Govinda who wears a garland of forest-tulasī! O Govinda who abides on the Śeṣādri hill, O Govinda who reposes on the cosmic Śeṣa! — Govindā Hari Govindā, Gōkulanandana Govindā, Vēṅkaṭaramaṇa Govindā.
contentBlocks.meanings: tirumalavāsa = dweller (vāsa) at Tirumala; tulasīvanamāla = one wearing a garland (māla) of tulasī from the garden/forest (vana) — tulasī (holy basil) is the most sacred plant of Vaiṣṇava devotion, said to be the earthly form of Lakṣmī and the most beloved offering to Viṣṇu; śeṣādrinilaya = one whose abode (nilaya) is Śeṣādri (the hill of Ādiśeṣa); śeṣaśāyinī = one who reclines (śāyin) on Śeṣa (Ananta, the cosmic serpent).
